Jun 11, 2026

## Adam x Andrew \- Website For Maxim

Invited [Andrew Rapier](mailto:andrewrapier@beatitat.com) [aveinfurt@maximconstruction.net](mailto:aveinfurt@maximconstruction.net)

Attachments [Adam x Andrew - Website For Maxim](https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?eid=Mjc3YTN2YnBycGgxcGN1NmliNnFpbmh0ZnYgYW5kcmV3cmFwaWVyQGJlYXRpdGF0LmNvbQ)

Meeting records [Transcript](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1o_RK45xu3PsLd6dxouhIHVqbz-LJFbHiAkSIgSXB4TI/edit?usp=drive_web&tab=t.enk8i217q0d4) 

### Summary

Discussion covered the website redesign and potential Customer Relationship Management implementation while finalizing billing and project priorities.

**Website Redesign and Structure**  
The transition from a single-page layout to a multi-page site was approved to better showcase services and credentials. Aesthetic adjustments and content restructuring will prioritize a professional industrial focus.

**Customer Relationship Management Integration**  
A custom web-based system was proposed to streamline incident reporting, work orders, and time tracking. This platform aims to replace fragmented communications with automated notification trails for maintenance tasks.

**Project Priorities and Billing**  
Leadership selected the 1-time payment model for the website launch and formally deferred Customer Relationship Management development until after the new site goes live.

### Decisions

## Aligned

* **Website design and content updates** The website design and content are updated to lighten the background image, reorganize the Services and Process page structures, remove redundant technical specifications, include a credentials section on the About page, and use the term "local skilled craftsmen" in the site copy.

* **View Blueprints portal repurposing** The "View Blueprints" section is repurposed into a bid and document access portal tailored for subcontractors.

* **Web-based platform preference** The development strategy for company solutions will remain web-based to ensure cross-platform compatibility for Android and iOS users instead of pursuing native application development.

* **Website launch project prioritization** The project sequence prioritizes the completion and launch of the website before proceeding with the development and integration of the CRM solution.

* **Initial project payment model selected** The initial project payment structure is set to a one-time fee, with the maintenance package and ongoing support to be discussed upon project completion.

* **Premium project scope and budget approved** The project scope and budget are finalized at $2,500 for the premium package, which includes four to five pages along with SEO and Google integration services.

* **Hosting cycle duration set** The website hosting agreement is established as a 12-month billing cycle at a rate of $75 per month.

We've **updated the Decisions section** using your feedback.

Let us know what you think: [Helpful](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_5p6FWBVWvynleNU?isGoogler=no&isHelpful=yes) or [Not Helpful](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_5p6FWBVWvynleNU?isGoogler=no&isHelpful=no)

### Next steps

- [ ] \[The group\] Send Logos: Provide existing company branding assets for web integration.

- [ ] \[Andrew Rapier\] Modify Website: Lighten the background image and update project description text to use surgical precision without redundant terminology.

- [ ] \[Andrew Rapier\] Update Site Structure: Reorganize services, process, and about sections as discussed.

- [ ] \[Andrew Rapier\] Add Credentials: Include relevant memberships and certifications in the company profile.

- [ ] \[The group\] Draft Content: Create descriptions about utilizing local skilled craftsmen for web publication.

- [ ] \[Andrew Rapier\] Develop Bid Portal: Implement a secure area for contractors to download prints and project specifications.

- [ ] \[Andrew Rapier\] Provide CRM Demonstrations: Share additional examples of Customer Relationship Management features relevant to maintenance and incident tracking.

- [ ] \[Andrew Rapier\] Send Invoice: Send the project invoice to accounting@maximconstruction.net with Adam copied on the email.

- [ ] \[Adam Gene\] Verify Email: Verify with Prefix Technologies whether the contact@maximconstruction.net email address is still active.

- [ ] \[Andrew Rapier\] Confirm Hosting: Confirm the hosting credentials and domain access based on the information provided for the website migration.

- [ ] \[Adam Gene\] Provide Website Content: Provide relevant photos and project highlights for the new website.

- [ ] \[Adam Gene\] Coordinate Payment: Discuss the potential for a special check run to cover the invoice for the website project.

### Details

*Did the screenshots in this section make your notes [better](https://goo.gle/44IH7J9) or [worse](https://goo.gle/4b5ZWtJ)?*

* **Meeting Introduction and Shutdown Overview**: Andrew Rapier meets with Adam Gene and Wes to discuss the company's current activities and potential technical solutions ([00:00:41](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.ye8gi71rky40)). Adam Gene explains that the team is currently winding down two large industrial shutdowns, which involves regions or entire facilities—such as soap or oil plants—ceasing production to allow contractors to perform maintenance, upgrades, and equipment installation ([00:01:26](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.cobrcttiwt1r)).

* **Website Mock Feedback and Aesthetic Adjustments**: Adam Gene reviews the website mock created by Andrew Rapier and expresses satisfaction with the layout but requests specific aesthetic changes ([00:04:37](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.8vgvseepn774)). The requested adjustments include lightening the background image of the structure to make it less dark and modifying the copy to avoid redundant uses of the word "industrial," with a preference for phrasing like "surgical precision" ([00:06:03](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.gl4h5y1s78m)).

* **Website Structure and Functionality**: Andrew Rapier discusses the benefits of moving from a single-page brochure website to a multi-page site to better showcase company services and authority ([00:08:42](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.o4thv4atbgj2)). They agree that a multi-page structure is more appropriate for a construction company working with industrial plants and major corporations than a simple one-page layout ([00:10:36](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.11ff4szhn0ug)).

* **Services Page Organization**: Adam Gene requests a restructure of the services page, suggesting quick links for Construction Management, Office Renovation, Factory Maintenance, and a "Specialty Skills" section to replace Special Projects. The goal is to provide a user-friendly layout with specific project blurbs, photos for each service, and a process page that outlines the workflow from consultation to project close-out ([00:11:44](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.y3hf61ro8x28)).

* **About Page and Credentials**: The team discusses the content for an About page, which will feature the company's history, specific memberships like ISN and KOKA, and its relationships with sister companies such as Western Heights Waterjet and Confab ([00:15:30](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.bfiq8xlcqi35)). They also plan to incorporate messaging about their reliance on local, skilled craftsmen to highlight their competitive edge ([00:17:10](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.4gca2qjyml0w)).

* **Website Metrics and Data**: Regarding the statistics displayed on the website, Adam Gene suggests moving away from the "current load capacity" figure in favor of tracking managed assets. Potential replacements discussed include the number of ongoing plant maintenance programs or the combined 200,000 square feet of fabrication capabilities shared across their sister companies ([00:18:28](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.463bln4tol85)).

* **Subcontractor Portal and Document Access**: Andrew Rapier and Adam Gene decide to repurpose the "View Blueprints" feature into a dedicated subcontractor bid page. This portal will allow authorized contractors to access project-specific documents, prints, and specifications for upcoming or active projects, such as the current project at Grants Farm, facilitating a smoother bidding process ([00:21:37](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.nn9rroxahca9)).

* **CRM Platform Overview and Capabilities**: Andrew Rapier demonstrates a custom CRM solution tailored for property management and construction ([00:23:47](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.yf7lqfj2ybpy)). The system supports distinct portals for administrators, employees, and clients, allowing for streamlined tracking of work orders, daily logs, and incident reports, which Andrew Rapier notes helps maintain accountability and transparency ([00:27:06](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.v76anjyltawx)).

  ![][image1]

* **Technical Framework of the CRM**: Andrew Rapier confirms the CRM is a web-based application, a preference supported by Adam Gene due to the company's mix of Android and iOS mobile devices ([00:26:03](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.11crp6d0c4r)). The platform is custom-coded, which allows Andrew Rapier to integrate specific features like invoice approval tracking, photo uploads for receipts, and detailed project reporting without relying on third-party limitations ([00:29:04](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.a6garg7j9023)) ([00:31:21](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.ynnoyno3hcv6)).

* **Integration of Time Tracking and Maintenance**: Adam Gene discusses the potential to transition away from their current time-tracking app, Typhoon, and integrate these functions into the CRM ([00:33:24](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.jclzmvo5od9c)). The proposed system would allow employees to clock in and out, and for management to assign tasks related to specific maintenance processes, such as machine changeovers at facilities like Luxco ([00:34:44](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.wx35n164ye32)) ([00:38:31](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.cbg3qr5na5tz)).

* **Automated Incident and Work Order Reporting**: The CRM will allow employees to generate incident reports or work orders during daily site walkthroughs, which automatically notifies the administrator and the relevant parties ([00:39:43](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.4utd6zij3l75)). Adam Gene highlights that this capability is crucial for tracking reactive maintenance—such as repairs needed after equipment damage—and ensuring a complete paper trail of activities ([00:38:31](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.cbg3qr5na5tz)).

* **Automated Notification Trails for Parts**: Andrew Rapier and Adam Gene discuss using the CRM to create automated notification systems for critical tasks, such as ordering specific parts or equipment components. This feature would trigger alerts to all responsible parties as soon as an issue is logged, ensuring instant awareness and reducing the reliance on handwritten notes or fragmented email chains ([00:44:57](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.heykfeded96r)).

* **CRM Implementation Strategy**: Adam Gene clarifies that while they are currently implementing proposal and quote workflows into QuickBooks, they prefer to prioritize the CRM for project and plant maintenance tracking ([00:49:06](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.ldugwfdgejf7)). They agree that the CRM project will be a subsequent phase, following the successful launch of the new company website ([00:53:08](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.7xw4ceh1km58)).

  ![][image2]

* **Project Next Steps and Access**: Andrew Rapier commits to prioritizing the website launch and then developing a CRM mock-up for review ([00:53:08](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.7xw4ceh1km58)). Adam Gene provides the necessary account information for the hosting platform, Squarespace, and credentials for the maximumconstruction.net domain, allowing Andrew Rapier to move forward with the website transition ([00:55:08](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.lp0qz7unkni3)).

* **Website Project Overview**: Adam Gene and Andrew Rapier discussed the need to redesign the Maximum Construction website, citing dissatisfaction with the current site's performance and appearance. Adam Gene tasked Andrew Rapier with providing technical solutions and expertise to replace the current system, prioritizing functionality over maintaining the existing, outdated setup ([00:59:04](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.ixg8aphfuxi9)).

* **Billing Models**: Andrew Rapier presented two billing options for the website development: a continuous managed option with a monthly retainer, or a one-time payment structure split into two equal payments, half upfront and half upon completion ([00:59:56](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.ve67stx6ql5z)). Adam Gene selected the one-time payment model for the current project, with the possibility of transitioning to a maintenance package once the site is live ([01:00:56](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.5r09fts48hyr)).

* **Website Scope and Integration**: The project scope was defined to include approximately four to five pages: a landing page, services (incorporating project highlights), process, about, and a "view blueprints" page, which includes future integration with SharePoint. The "view blueprints" page may initially be left empty to facilitate future development ([01:01:55](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.4d8oc3kvmh2c)).

* **Project Pricing and SEO Services**: Andrew Rapier provided a quote of $2500 for the premium development option, which covers the website pages, SEO implementation, and Google page integration. The timeline for the project is estimated at two to four weeks ([01:03:11](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.b7jcxnbbtglw)).

* **Payment Logistics**: Adam Gene instructed that all invoices be sent to accounting@maximumconstruction.net, with Adam Gene copied on the correspondence, to ensure the billing is processed efficiently during the company's check runs ([01:04:13](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.lo4nqakrdqz2)).

* **Hosting Agreement**: The hosting agreement was established at a rate of $75 per month, covering server costs, images, and uptime management. Adam Gene approved a 12-month billing cycle for these hosting services ([01:05:07](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.qd8us9pd03tv)).

* **Communication and Content Handover**: Andrew Rapier noted that they are available for emergencies and direct communication via phone, and will include details on ongoing development in the invoice for future reference ([01:06:02](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.a5lv4jz7j9p6)). Adam Gene agreed to provide relevant photos and project content to be highlighted on the website ([01:06:58](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.ak1or536v7iq)).

* **Email and IT Integration**: The conversation concluded with a discussion on email infrastructure, specifically regarding the contact@maximumconstruction.net address used for form submissions ([01:06:58](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.ak1or536v7iq)). Adam Gene plans to contact Prefix Technologies, the company’s IT provider, to determine if this email is active or if a new setup is required to direct incoming inquiries to the team ([01:07:49](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.lyswswf9pxb7)). Andrew Rapier suggested creating a functional business email address if the previous one is unavailable to ensure contact form submissions are routed correctly to the appropriate personnel ([01:08:45](?tab=t.enk8i217q0d4#heading=h.8evyggn0drv6)).

*You should review Gemini's notes to make sure they're accurate. [Get tips and learn how Gemini takes notes](https://support.google.com/meet/answer/14754931)*
